key_insight: A detailed finite element model was used to iteratively refine the WST altitude structure so it can safely support the mirrors while minimizing weight and resonance risk.
engineering_relevance: The work provides structural design criteria for a large telescope support frame, directly informing stiffness, stress, mass, and vibration requirements in precision observatory engineering.
method: Built an FE model of the steel altitude structure with detailed beams, plates, and representative boundary conditions.
Evaluated structural weight, deformations, stresses, and modal/frequency response.
Used preliminary results to optimize cross sections and shell thickness toward a refined final configuration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the model was used to estimate weight, mechanical deformations, stresses, and frequency response, and that these outputs drove structural optimization.
watch_item: Results depend on the accuracy of the assumed boundary conditions and preliminary section properties, so hardware validation will be important before final fabrication.
paper_title: WST, the Wide-field Spectroscopic Telescope: Telescope structure FE analyses
